ops.text:
RESULTS E33 TEST CLOSE + VERIFY (VERIFY-confirmed to the digit, all integrity audits pass — no test leakage, isolated=true scaffolded ~50K/call confirmed, 170 keys = full manifest, no subsetting; total spend $91.11 under the $100 cap). DECISION = NULL (mechanical): rubric_4 = 0.8588 (146/170) vs baseline v6 = 0.8235 (140/170), gain +3.53pt (≥3 MET), but exact McNemar p=0.263 (b=13 rubric-right, c=7 v6-right, only 20 discordant pairs — NOT <0.05). Over-flag (the designed target): baseline 25 → winner 14, nearly halved; under-flag 5 → 10 (the rubric trades over-flag for under-flag, which is why net gain shrank from +8.33 dev to +3.53 test).

DISPOSITION (orchestrator): E33 is instrument-limited, not method-limited — a real, VERIFY-confirmed, on-target behavioral improvement (over-flag halved) that the frozen n=170 / 20-discordant-pair frame cannot certify at α=0.05. This is a textbook instance of the campaign's core thesis (cf E30.1: these frames are underpowered). Therefore:
1. rubric_4 is recorded as a DEPLOYMENT CANDIDATE (not a shipped change, not the 162a5dc triage-prompt slot yet) — its true test is the E30.2b live-traffic anytime-valid monitor over the ~190/wk accrual, where the over-flag reduction can accumulate significance the frozen frame can't provide. The rubric text + its per-row test behavior are the handoff artifact.
2. E35 (GEPA) stays DEFERRED — and is now DOWNGRADED to low-expected-value: another prompt method optimized against the SAME underpowered n=170 frame faces the same ceiling (a clear +8.3pt-dev rubric already came up short on test). The higher-value spend is the instrument work (E30.2a cross-fit MDE 0.0167, E30.2b live monitor) that would let BOTH the current rubric and any future prompt change be certified. E35 remains available if the user wants the breadth, but I recommend the monitor path over spending ~$60-75 on it.
3. E34 stays deferred (unchanged).
The rubric's mechanism is worth preserving regardless: a fault-existence gate (a corrective category requires a concrete named artifact it faults/reverses; forward guardrails, course-changes without blame, relayed external state, obstacle reports = noise) directly attacks the over-flag bias E30.3 measured. Artifacts: ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E33/{prompts/rubric_4.txt, out/test.*.jsonl, results.json, report.md, verify/}.
